fields.Name: iMIS
fields.Description: iMIS EMS (Advanced Solutions International) REST API connector for associations and nonprofits. Supports parties, memberships, events, fundraising, invoicing, and commerce via iMIS REST API with OAuth2 password-grant Bearer token authentication.

documentedAlternate.note: Logged-in session (browser/cookie based) uses a RequestVerificationToken anti-CSRF header. Not suitable for machine-to-machine connector use. OAuth2 password grant is the documented M2M path.

Pagination.hasNextNote: No explicit boolean HasNext field. Pagination termination inferred: if Count < Limit, no more pages. If Count == Limit, advance offset by Limit and fetch next page.

Discovery.note: Per-tenant configurable Business Objects are enumerated at runtime via the auth-gated EntityDefinition endpoint; the build-time Declared set is the docs-provable core subset.

OutOfScopeObjectFamilies: CustomBusinessObjects: Per-tenant custom Business Objects defined in the iMIS back-office. Discoverable only at runtime via auth-gated GET /BOEntityDefinition endpoint. The public OpenAPI specs contain BOEntityDefinitionData (the discovery mechanism) but not the custom BO field schemas themselves. These become Discovered rows at runtime.

IQAQueryResults: Per-tenant IQA (iMIS Query Architect) saved query result schemas. Each tenant defines custom report queries with custom column sets. QueryServiceData lists the queries but individual result schemas require auth-gated runtime discovery via GET /QueryService/{queryName}.

PanelDefinitions: Per-tenant iPart panel definitions with custom configurations. PanelDefinitionData and PanelSourceSummaryData provide the discovery endpoints but the content/schema of custom panels is tenant-configured and only enumerable at runtime.
Configuration.OutOfScopeScopeReason: iMIS is highly configurable — every deployment has its own schema of tenant-defined custom business objects (extensible entities, user-defined tables). The StaticMetadata (Declared) scope covers ONLY the 40+ documented core / standard BOs that ship with iMIS (Party/Person/Organization/ComboParty, Event/EventRegistration, Membership, Commerce, Fundraising, Communication/Activity, Group, IQA, Media, Batch, Metadata endpoints). These are provable from public developer documentation. The per-tenant custom BO long tail (fields, objects, composed entities, IQA queries) is unknown at build time and becomes a RuntimeDiscovered scope filled by the connector's auth-gated EntityDefinition introspection endpoint and per-object DiscoverFields calls at deployment. The generic /api/{EntityName} + {id} + Offset/Limit pagination + UpdatedOn watermark pattern and the CRUD (POST/PUT/DELETE) surface are tenant-agnostic and framework-level; per-object writability and incremental-field coverage are proven at runtime via the live integration creation pipeline.
Configuration.APIVersioningNote: iMIS REST API version is encoded in the base URL path. Current stable version: /v1/ prefix (e.g. https://{tenant}.imis.com/api/v1/{EntityName}). Host is always tenant-specific.

universalPK.note: iMIS core objects consistently use an Id field as primary key. Per-object PK confirmation required at runtime via EntityDefinition endpoint since the field name may vary for custom BOs.

Configuration.IncrementalSyncNote: Core objects (Party, Person, Organization, Event, EventRegistration, Membership, etc.) support incremental sync via an UpdatedOn or LastUpdated timestamp field. The watermark field name varies per object and must be confirmed per-object via docs or runtime discovery.
Configuration.WebhooksNote: iMIS does not expose a public webhook/event subscription API in its documented REST API surface. Event-driven integration is not available via the public developer docs.
Configuration.BulkOperationsNote: No documented bulk batch endpoint. Individual record CRUD only. iMIS REST API uses per-record POST/PUT/DELETE.
Configuration.TokenRefreshNote: OAuth2 password grant does not issue refresh tokens in the iMIS documented flow. Re-acquire a new Bearer token by re-executing the password grant on 401 Unauthorized. Tokens have a documented expiry (session-scoped).

ErrorResponseShape.description: iMIS REST API returns standard HTTP status codes. Error bodies follow the iMIS API error structure with a Message field and optional ExceptionMessage and ExceptionType for server errors.

WriteCapability.note: iMIS REST API documents POST (Create), PUT (Update), and DELETE operations on core business objects. Write capability is object-dependent and confirmed per-object at runtime. Some objects may be read-only.

Configuration.ConcurrencyControlNote: No ETag, If-Match, or version field documented in the iMIS public REST API. Conflict resolution must use snapshot-3-way approach; MostRecent is not safely applicable.
Configuration.DeleteSemanticsNote: iMIS typically archives/deactivates records rather than hard-deleting them. Party and membership records are set to inactive status. Actual semantics are object-dependent. Some object families may support hard delete via DELETE verb. Verify per-object before enabling DeleteBehavior.
Configuration.DefaultSyncDirectionNote: Read-only (Pull) recommended as default for all iMIS objects. Bidirectional sync requires sandbox validation. Write paths (POST/PUT/DELETE) exist in the API but must be tested against a sandbox before enabling.
Configuration.DefaultConflictResolutionNote: No ETag or version field documented (ConcurrencyControl=none). MostRecent is not safely applicable. DestWins is the conservative default for bidirectional if enabled. Validate conflict behavior in sandbox before changing.
